item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally don't like SCPs that is an explanation of actually history.
I like the execution and idea, but I have a thing against this kind of topic.
No votes from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a very original take on both its concepts, the still-living Hitler and the regenerating food of the Norse gods. Upvoted.

One thing that I think that would make sense would be a little note on why the soviets didn't turned it into a weapon afterwards. And there are several possible explanations.

First of all, with the anomaly needing a skeleton that doesn't regenerate to serve as basis, it is fairly possible that it was far less effective in comparison to the cost. With post-WWII warfare, the fact that it regenerates flesh isn't by itself such a huge advantage if the soldier do not have other actual advantages, and that seems to be the case. They would have resistance to pain, but a normal soldier with painkiller treatment is far more cheap.

Maybe there was a reason those creatures were just used as food sources. And even as food sources it could easily be too costly to result in a reliable source for great populations.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
Congratulations on hitting the 3 digit mark. This SCP just happened to pop up on my recommended viewing on youtube today and I decided to check it out. While I prefer longer articles, I think this one does alright. I have really only two nitpicks, which I would propose fixing.

1. The tag says sapient. But there's no evidence for this, in the article. I'd propose adding in a line somewhere, probably in the SCP Description that explains it's sapient. Alternatively in the containment procedures, maybe mention the Foundation providing the SCP with means of entertainment? Such as books or television?

2. Clarifying if it is sentient, that it possesses Hitler's personality and memories, or is merely an innocent anomaly that was built to be purposefully and needlessly punished by the idiot madman running the soviet union. SCP-2430 was described as dead inside, but I thought that might have meant his organs, but could also mean his mind.

3. Bonus: Not needed, but if you do make it sapient, maybe expand with an interview. It could even be noted that it does not sound like the original Hitler, but has maybe picked up a Russian accent? From it's years with the GRU Division "P" which might have made Stalin particularly angry with him.
Just realised that it has reached three digits. It had a slight bumpy ride at its embryonic stage, but it became the hit that it deserved to be. Here's to it continuing to stir readers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(tho might wanna change the title, probably. or at least explain that the german thought it was a clone or something)

As far OBSCURA is concerned, they think that SCP-2430 is the actual Hitler who was badly traumatised/tortured by Soviet capture.

I am sticking with the current title, since it is more of a out-of-universe allusion to the trope of cloning Hitler (which has kindly referenced this very article).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this was almost "lols, we cloned hitler for the lols' but the fact it isnt, saves it

+1
(tho might wanna change the title, probably. or at least explain that the german thought it was a clone or something)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
but see, that could be used on an SCP of its OWN, just remove "hitler" from this and this can be an actual original SCP

I would argue that the 'Hitler' part is needed, since it would be playing up the trope of surviving Nazis (until I destroyed that expectation to some degree). If it were not moulded into the appearance of a certain infamous dictator, the article would have been much weaker.

It would just be a generic blob of flesh that regenerates.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was hoping I would like this article but it has nothing to do with hitler….its literally just an empty "husk" with no functioning organs. I did like his biological immortality, though. how he just cant die no matter what they attempt…

but see, that could be used on an SCP of its OWN, just remove "hitler" from this and this can be an actual original SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't think a Hitler clone thing would actually intrigue me this much. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconding this question two years after the fact. Although, I will point out that this has the "sapient" tag, which implies the former rather than the latter.

Which is its own sort of horrifying. If it's a sapient being, does it think it's Hitler? Does it have his memories or any sort of connection to him besides the superficial? If it's sapient but has no real connection to Hitler, its existence is absolutely horrifying to think about. If it does think it's Hitler and has reason to believe so beyond its appearance, its continued existence is horrifying in an entirely different way.

item.title: Re: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Much appreciated Wrong, you saved me some paperwork :P

item.title: Re: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: EDIT: Actually, for greater efficiency, I will do the honours then.

item.title: Re: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Understood. Would you like to make the edit yourself?

item.title: Re: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Ideally, a page that encapsulates OBSKURA would be preferable. But since that does not exist, I would go for a footnote approach.

As for Conwell's Tale, it is set when OBSKURA was still Obskurakorps.

Therefore, I would like the crosslink to be this instead (changing only Footnote 1):

1. A Group of Interest regarded as a successor of the Thule Society and Ahnenerbe Obskurakorps, it originated as the esoteric arm of ODESSA prior to its eventual defection from ODESSA upon acquisition of several anomalous objects. Other anomalous objects related to OBSKURA and its predecessors include SCP-2893, SCP-3878.

item.title: Re: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: You could also use SCP-2893 and/or Conwell's Aboard the Train to Berlin for the Ahnenerbe Obskurakorps in the footnote.

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Edit: Edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show proposed edits
- Hide

To link to other Obskura content, I propose the following edit:

SCP-2430 was recovered in Buenos Aires, Argentina on ██/██/1960 in the residence of the late █████ ████████, who is associated with OBSKURA.

to

SCP-2430 was recovered in Buenos Aires, Argentina on ██/██/1960 in the residence of the late █████ ████████, who is associated with OBSKURA.

item.title: Staff Post/Author Post - Reversion Note (Open)
item.content:encoded:
Noting that I have reverted several edits made to this article by Dans890, pertaining to the inclusion of an image file. Non-authors are only allowed to make edits in terms of spelling, punctuation and grammar. Content changes (including adding or removing content from the article) requires contact and approval with the author.

User Dans890, please refrain from adding or removing content from articles you did not author. Please consult the author of any suggestions that might involve a change in content, and let the author decide if they are to be implemented.

And since I am actually the author here, my answer to this issue is: No. This article works fine without an image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could see a lot of people with the means to do it decide a quick death wasn't payback enough for what he did and trying to enact some form of "justice."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, MOST of the skeleton is still there, and it doesn't destroy the bones, just remove them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you had made it some GoI or other person doing this instead of Stalin, I think it would hold together.

I honestly doubt some GoI would actually do it. I at least imagine [INSERT GOI] to be smart enough to not use regenerating flesh in such a manner.

Rather than goofy, I would say it was an impulse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're not wrong. "Clone" just has a better ring to it and I wanted to prime readers into the "Hitler clone" trope before subverting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That seems weird to me. If someone had done this for no reason whatsoever, it wouldn't make any sense. Stalin and GRU-P have a reason, and the resources.

Maybe I am misreading, but are you saying it would be better is someone we never heard of did it for the EvLOLs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wanted to upvote but ultimately rescinded it. I can see why someone made an unkillable Hitler, but the fact is making Hitler in any form into a skip, and also including Stalin is just too goofy for words. If you had made it some GoI or other person doing this instead of Stalin, I think it would hold together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I still like this a lot, there is one question that I feel it should answer.

Does it have human intelligence? Or is it a pig-goat brain in a human-shaped body? The latter makes the most sense, but I don't see that it is clearly stated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's not a clone, okay, but it's a homonculus that looks like Hitler. Tell me if I'm missing something else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had an epiphany and originally wanted to write an SCP based on creatures in Norse myths that regrow their flesh… ant the rest is history.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Started to love the article after i read it through this point of view.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Better than the draft so no downvote there, but it still feels somewhat barebones. The notes don't really convince me on the article here, and it doesn't really bring me to care, ultimately. (I'm still just not into GRU-P so their presence here doesn't help.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MrWrong, how did you managed to pull the Hitler clone off, you monster.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That horrible thing isn't his brother anymore, so it's cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's… a really horrible thing to say about your brother…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew Hitler had escaped to Argentina! This is just additional proof that my brother got a Hitler brain transplant when he went there on vacation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and sweet: I dig it! +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In retrospect, what makes you think that Stalin wanted it? In the writing, did it depict him personally saying that he wants a Hitler-shaped punching bag?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I liked this quite a bit. I upvoted at "Modifications to its nervous system such that pain sensitivity is heightened."

The people downvoting because "it's Hitler," well, no it's not. The Argentinian Nazis just thought it was him. It's actually a pig-clone Hitler. It makes sense for the reasoning given. I honestly thought, "why hadn't someone done this yet?" when I read it, so great job.

I also like how Stalin wasn't doing it for petty revenge, he was doing it for Propaganda purposes. He didn't want to torture a fake Hitler doll, he wanted to show the world he had Hitler. This is exactly in character for him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I decided to simply be brief, I acknowledge that you did good research. However, I had other issues such as why Stalin would want Hitler as a punching/torture bag. In fact, Stalin (and the rest of the Allies) went through a lot of trouble to be certain that Hitler was actually gone.

Also, I'm wasn't aiming to sound like a scientist and I'm not the only one who has called it a clone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: True, but that will be quite mainstream. I'd figure that GRU-P would already have tons of other anomalous weapons ravaged throughout the Union and from territories they had liberated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My biggest issue is that it doesn't seem in character, even for Stalin, to pass up the opportunity for indestructible supersoldiers in favor of a hitler clone. We are talking about the guy that greenlit human-ape hybrid research because he wanted an army of intelligent ape-men.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Point taken, and that's why I'm not in Med School or Biology.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have tissue from an edible, infinitely regenerating beast.
Do you:
A) Study how to cultivate it and ensure an eternal food supply for your starving people
B) Create an army of unstoppable killing machines (for purely defensive purposes, of course)
C) Make meat puppets of your favorite past enemies and eat popcorn while having them repeatedly executed in creative ways.

Yep. Totally see Stalin picking option "C".
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, here's something that threw me off a bit. It says its regeneration doesn't apply to its bones, but it says it can heal amputations and decapitation. Shouldn't it be unable to regenerate the parts of its bones (and its entire skull) removed in an amputation? Or does it grow all the flesh back but not the removed bone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. I think I'll upvote. It isn't an anomalous justification for Hitler or the Nazis, it doesn't make light of their crimes, and is a bizarre way of explaining what actually happened to his body… in contrast with the usual "him getting frozen and hidden in a secret bunker";p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not so much USSR, but Stalin. This whole operation is Stalin's plan (Project Judecca); GRU-P originally proposed a smarter use of self-healing flesh puppets (Project Einherjar).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you ask me, had they applied the flesh of Sæhrímnir properly (read: Project Einherjar), GRU-P would have an army of unstoppable flesh soldiers for human wave tactics. It will be no end boss, but a never-ending attack wave (where one will die due to attrition in the end).

As it is, it is a very annoying and screechy wall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting, it's kinda like the Emperor Joker plot where Batman winds up killed over many times.

But I would recall an alternate history fiction involving Stalin keeping Hitler as a "pet" underneath the Kremlin as his trophy, which directed me accordingly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would work better as a Wolfenstein end boss. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"vestigial" typically refers organs and structures that have become shrunken and unusable over time through mutation, so if the internal anatomy is non-anomalous then "vestigial" wouldn't quite be the right word.

Point taken. Let's settle for "functionless".

P.S. Don't worry about it. Let's move on from there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted. Making a clone of Hitler to punish him sounds like what the USSR are willing to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for paraphrasing the title (before I even set it in the mainlist), except for the fact it's not actually Hitler and it's not what scientists would define as clone.

In biology, cloning is the process of producing similar populations of genetically identical individuals

It's more accurately a mass of flesh from magical boars and goats that is moulded to resemble a certain person, with no genetic connection between SCP-2430 and the person it is modelled after.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted.

This actually reminds me of a soviet movie I seen a short bit of. The gist there was that for a million murders, one death isn't enough - so there was a facility whose purpose was revealed as either cloning or ressurecting Hitler and executing him over and over again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But the part involving Hitler…

I can understand the sentiments addressed, since I used to groan at another SCP with Nazi wunderwaffe.

I did contemplate using another figure (e.g. Emperor Showa,1 Qin Hui2 or similar figures who were not "held account (sufficiently) for their crimes"), but Hitler flowed the most naturally. At the baseline, the SCP is a Nordic artefact and the back-and-forth between Nazi Germany and USSR worked out as the best in my mind.

It is fully probable that the meat puppet could be made to look like anyone else (there are like tonnes of it lying around), so if it were acquired by certain people, other things could happen.

Footnotes
1. Who was relieved of all responsibility in WWII despite him maybe not being completely oblivious to the war
2. Who was known for backstabbing Yue Fei and have a statue to be spat upon

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neutral but leaning to downvote. I like the writing. I like everything else. Hell, I like the part involving Stalin. But the part involving Hitler… its been done in so many mediums before and so recognizable. If you changed things up, removed the Hitler… it is a weird cultural bias too. Westerners will freak out over Hitler used in such a way but less known dictators in other parts of the world? I doubt any would complain. I know I'm giving into that double standard but I don't see this working out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, having pulled my head out of my ass and actually read the article, I do like it, +1. Just one minor nitpick from a biology major:

organs are functionless and exist in a vestigial state

"vestigial" typically refers organs and structures that have become shrunken and unusable over time through mutation, so if the internal anatomy is non-anomalous then "vestigial" wouldn't quite be the right word.

+ show block
– hide block

And again, sorry for being a huge shitlord the other day

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's an immortal clone of Hitler.

-1

item.title: Thanks, Attribution, and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
Firstly, thanks to reviewers such as kaktus, Decibelles, sirpudding, Zyn and DreamwalkerFae. Especial thanks to Dr Solo since he did "bring" more reviewers than I would usually muster via forum, and sped up the process.

Secondly, credit's due where they're due.

  • OBSKURA is taken from the GOC Canon Hub, which is compiled by Clef.
  • The Rite of Nyúz is a concept from Metaphysician's sandbox on Sarkicism, which is described as the "methodical removal of portions of the body over an extended period of time, eventually resulting in death."

This article features: Sarkcism Hub, as compiled by Metaphysician

+ show block
– hide block

SCP-2430 is effectively a combination of Sæhrímnir, Tanngrisnir and Tanngnjóstr – three creatures in Norse myths that can regenerate their flesh.

Between building an army of unstoppable flesh things and making a meat puppet of someone you hate, which would you choose?

And as for the "Uralic flesh-crafters"… Sarkicists, much?
